COE-307: Normalize LiteLLM request data into canonical request tables (#20)

* feat(normalization): implement request normalizer job with reconciliation reporting

Summary:
- Create normalize_requests.py with RequestNormalizer and RequestNormalizerJob classes
- Implement ReconciliationReport for tracking unmapped rows with actionable diagnostics
- Add UnmappedRowDiagnostics for detailed per-row failure analysis
- Wire normalization job into CLI with `benchmark normalize run` and `benchmark normalize report` commands
- Export new classes from collectors package
- Add comprehensive unit tests (30 tests covering normalization, reporting, and job execution)

Rationale:
- LiteLLM request data needs normalization into canonical Request ORM entities
- Session correlation keys (experiment_id, variant_id, etc.) must be preserved in metadata
- Idempotent bulk insert via SQLRequestRepository prevents duplicates
- Reconciliation report surfaces unmapped rows with missing field counts and error categories
- CLI integration enables operator-driven normalization workflows

Implementation Details:
- RequestNormalizer handles timestamp parsing (ISO format, Unix epoch, multiple field names)
- Supports latency extraction (seconds->ms conversion, multiple field variants)
- Token counts extracted from nested usage object or top-level fields
- Error detection from error field or response.error
- Cache hit detection from cache_hit or cached fields
- Correlation keys searched in both top-level and nested metadata

Tests:
- All 30 new normalization tests pass
- All 210 existing tests pass
- Lint clean on all new files

fix(demo): Address PR #10 feedback - fix demo_session_service.py runtime errors (#21)

* COE-305: Implement repository layer and benchmark metadata services

## Repository Layer (src/benchmark_core/repositories/)

New SQLAlchemy-based repositories for all canonical entities:
- base.py: AbstractRepository, SQLAlchemyRepository, RepositoryError hierarchy
- provider_repository.py: SQLProviderRepository with model management
- variant_repository.py: SQLVariantRepository with filtering by provider/harness
- experiment_repository.py: SQLExperimentRepository with variant associations
- task_card_repository.py: SQLTaskCardRepository with search capabilities
- harness_profile_repository.py: SQLHarnessProfileRepository
- session_repository.py: SQLSessionRepository with safe session creation/finalization
- request_repository.py: SQLRequestRepository with idempotent batch operations

## Service Layer (src/benchmark_core/services/)

New services for benchmark metadata management:
- session_service.py: Safe session lifecycle (create/finalize) with validation
- provider_service.py: Provider configuration management with model aliases
- variant_service.py: Variant definition and benchmark tags
- experiment_service.py: Experiment grouping and variant associations
- task_card_service.py: Task definition management
- harness_profile_service.py: Harness configuration with env rendering
- credential_service.py: Session-scoped proxy credential issuance
- benchmark_metadata_service.py: Unified service for cross-entity operations

## Key Features

- Referential integrity preserved via explicit FK checks and error handling
- Duplicate identifier rejection with DuplicateIdentifierError
- Safe session lifecycle: create with validation, finalize atomically
- Idempotent batch request ingestion for collectors
- Full test coverage: 52 new tests, all passing (129 total)

Renamed existing files for backward compatibility:
- repositories.py -> repositories_abc.py
- services.py -> services_abc.py

Summary:

These fixes address outstanding critical feedback from PR #10 that was merged
before all runtime issues were resolved.

commit.message:
fix(doctor): Remove automatic rehydration; add explicit CLI rehydration command (#61)

* Simplify conversation resumption by removing brittle rehydration logic

This change removes the complex LLM configuration drift detection and
conversation rehydration logic that was causing issues on every orchestrator
restart. Instead, we now adopt a simpler "dumb" approach that just reuses
conversations as-is, similar to how the OpenHands CLI behaves.

Key changes:

1. Removed LLM config fingerprint comparison and drift detection
   - LlmConfigFingerprint now only tracks model name for observability
   - Removed api_key_hash and base_url_hash fields
   - Removed sha2 hashing dependency

2. Removed rehydration methods:
   - rehydrate_session_for_llm_config_change
   - rehydrate_existing_session
   - build_rehydration_context

3. Removed conversation summary generation before rehydration:
   - Removed extract_message_history
   - Removed build_summarization_prompt
   - Removed generate_conversation_summary
   - Removed preserved_conversation_summary field from manifest

4. Removed token preservation logic for rehydration

5. Simplified try_resume_existing_session to just attach directly:
   - No more checking current vs stored LLM config
   - No more deleting and recreating conversations
   - Just attach to existing conversation_id and use stored config

6. Updated test expectations:
   - Renamed test to issue_session_runner_reuses_conversation_despite_llm_config_changes
   - Now expects continuation prompt instead of full prompt
   - Expects conversation reuse (not fresh) despite config changes
   - Verifies original API key is preserved in stored config

7. Updated documentation across 4 files to reflect simplified behavior

The previous rehydration logic was brittle because it would detect false
positives on every orchestrator restart due to subtle differences in how
LLM configs were resolved. This caused conversations to be unnecessarily
deleted and recreated, losing conversation history and requiring complex
recovery logic with Linear workpad context.

The new approach trusts that the stored conversation configuration in
meta.json is valid and simply reuses the conversation as-is. This is more
reliable and matches user expectations from the OpenHands CLI experience.

* Add explicit, decoupled conversation rehydration capability

This commit adds explicit rehydration functionality that is decoupled from the
normal conversation resumption flow. Rehydration is NOT automatically triggered
- it must be explicitly called when truly needed (e.g., corruption, export/import).

Changes:

1. RehydrationResult struct
   - Contains the new session, context for the prompt, and old conversation ID
   - Provides a clean API for accessing rehydration results

2. RehydrationOptions struct
   - Configurable options for rehydration (reason, summarization, max events)
   - Default: summarize=true, max_summary_events=50

3. IssueSessionRunner::rehydrate_conversation() method
   - Explicitly callable method to rehydrate a conversation
   - Creates fresh session with current configuration
   - Optionally summarizes old conversation history
   - Preserves token metrics through the manifest (already worked)

4. build_rehydration_context()
   - Builds context summarizing the old conversation history
   - Includes conversation metadata (ID, model, status)
   - Provides structured context for the new conversation

5. summarize_conversation()
   - Extracts key events from old conversation (messages, observations, errors)
   - Configurable maximum events to include
   - Formats as readable summary for context

6. UnexpectedEarlyResult error variant
   - New error type for when rehydration returns early result unexpectedly

7. lib.rs exports
   - Exported RehydrationResult and RehydrationOptions for public API

Design Decisions:
- Rehydration is opt-in, not automatic - avoids false positives on orchestrator restart
- Normal flow simply reuses conversations as-is (the "dumb" approach)
- Rehydration available for specific scenarios: corruption, export/import, etc.
- When rehydrating, optionally summarizes old conversation and provides context
- Token metrics preserved through manifest (no special handling needed)

This addresses the root cause where automatic rehydration was causing issues
on every orchestrator restart by making rehydration an explicit, intentional
operation rather than an automatic side effect of config comparison.

* fix(doctor): Make --rehydrate recreate all conversations with current API key

The doctor --rehydrate command was only rehydrating conversations that failed
to attach (404 or other errors). However, conversations that attached
successfully but had invalid/stale API keys were skipped. This left many
conversations with broken auth that would fail on first LLM call.

Changes:
- Doctor --rehydrate now unconditionally rehydrates ALL conversations
- Added API key fingerprint tracking to detect key changes in future
- Fixed token count preservation during rehydration
- Fixed apply_runtime_snapshot to not overwrite preserved token counts
- Fixed supervisor lifecycle to persist through rehydration

This ensures that when OH_SECRET_KEY or LLM_API_KEY changes, running
`opensymphony doctor --rehydrate` will fix all conversations by recreating
them with the current API key from environment

* fix(tests): Update test to expect reset when API key changes

* style: cargo fmt

* style: fix clippy warnings in TUI (manual_clamp, let_and_return)

* fix(doctor): Rehydrate all conversations unconditionally with current API key

- Fixed apply_runtime_snapshot() to conditionally update token counts
  (only if stream has higher values, preventing reset to 0)
- Fixed accumulate_tokens() to properly read accumulated totals from conversation state
- Added API key fingerprint tracking for detecting LLM config drift
- Updated test to expect conversation reset on API key change
- Fixed collapsible_if clippy warning in test

* style: cargo fmt

* fix: resolve clippy warnings blocking CI

Summary:
- Prefix unused conversation_id variable with underscore in test
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] to unused test helpers
- Simplify redundant closures in workspace config builders
- Move helper functions and imports before test module to fix
  items-after-test-module lint

Rationale:
Clippy was failing with -D warnings on:
- unused_variables in issue_session_runner test
- dead_code for StaticWorkpadCommentSource and workpad_comment helpers
- redundant_closure in four HookDefinition::shell calls
- items_after_test_module for run_rehydrate_command and helpers

Tests:
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (passes)
- cargo test --workspace (all 250+ tests pass)
- cargo fmt --check (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: delete old conversation on API key drift to prevent resource leak

When API key fingerprint differs between stored manifest and current
environment, delete the old conversation before returning Reset. This
prevents orphaned conversations when API keys rotate.

* fix: remove API key fingerprint drift detection

Simplified conversation resumption: no longer detect or handle API key
changes automatically. Conversations are reused as-is with their stored
config. If API key is actually invalid, attach will fail naturally and
explicit rehydration via CLI can be used.

- Removed fingerprint comparison logic from try_reuse_session()
- Updated test to expect conversation reuse despite API key changes

* docs: add rehydration documentation

- Added rehydration section to testing-and-operations.md with:
  - When to use rehydration
  - Command examples (single issue, bulk, with options)
  - How rehydration works
  - Difference between normal resumption and rehydration
- Added rehydrate commands to README.md quick start

* style: cargo fmt

* test: add rehydrate_conversation test coverage per PR review

Add comprehensive test `rehydrate_conversation_deletes_old_and_creates_new_with_token_preservation` that verifies:
- Old conversation is deleted from server (404 verified)
- New conversation is created with different ID
- Token counts (input/output/cache) are preserved across rehydration

Also refactors run_doctor_rehydration() return type from Vec<RehydrationAttemptResult>
to (usize, usize) tuple for cleaner success/failure reporting.

commit.message:
fix(jumia): relax OAuth token schema to match Vendor Center API (#922)

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

commit.message:
Fix redis repro issues (#163)

* Fix replay shadow stack desync causing 0/10 reproduction (defect #15)

_ReplayDporScheduler.report_and_wait() was ignoring the frame argument,
so _process_opcode was never called during replay. This left the shadow
stack empty, causing _call_might_report_access to skip CALL scheduling
points for C methods on mutable objects (list.append, dict.update, etc.).
The schedule desynchronised and the critical interleaving was never
enforced during reproduction.

Fix: call _process_opcode in the replay report_and_wait before
_wait_for_turn. The _ReplayEngine is a no-op for access reporting so
this only updates the shadow stack.

Also filter _redis_replay_mode scheduling points to data commands only,
excluding connection-setup commands (AUTH, SELECT) that didn't create
scheduling points during exploration.

Affected libraries now reproduce 10/10:
- sendgrid (was 0/10), simplejwt (was 4/10), eth-ape (was 0/10),
  django-simple-history orphaned-history test (was 0/10)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make Redis replay use scheduler-owned IO anchors

* Tighten IO-anchored replay semantics

commit.message:
[raster] Guard direct SVG paint callbacks

The raster SVG image callback assumes hb_raster_paint_glyph()
seeded svg_font/svg_glyph state before the paint protocol
reaches HB_PAINT_IMAGE_FORMAT_SVG. Clients can also call
hb_font_paint_glyph_or_fail() directly with
hb_raster_paint_get_funcs(), which leaves svg_font unset and
crashes in hb_raster_svg_render().

Return false when the callback is reached without that
wrapper-only state, and cover the direct API path with a
regression test.

Testing: CCACHE_DISABLE=1 meson test -C build
Fixes https://github.com/harfbuzz/harfbuzz/security/advisories/GHSA-ggh9-cgg2-r4vr
Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[gpos] Guard cursive attachment reversal

Cursive attachment reversal clears the old child edge before
walking the existing chain and reinstalling reversed links.
If the chain distance does not stay in range for
attach_chain(), reinstalling the reversed edge can truncate
and leave a poisoned chain value.

Pass the buffer length into the reversal helper so it drops
out-of-bounds chains, and use a round-trip fit check before
storing the reversed distance. When the reversed distance does
not fit, leave the old edge detached instead of corrupting
the chain.

Testing: CCACHE_DISABLE=1 meson test -C build --print-errorlogs
Fixes https://issues.chromium.org/issues/496302307
Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
